title: Add Souza et al. 2025 + reorder refs chronologically

body:
today I realized that julia does not automatically fuse operations in FMA (fused-multiply-add) operations. 
FMA are typically more efficient than separately adding an multiplying because it requires less registers, and also performs only the truncation at the end, potentially increasing also accuracy.

I found this nice lightweight sciML package (MuladdMacro.jl) that recursively adds `muladd` to the code via a macro, so I was keen to test.
I think this might reduce significantly our register usage, and in term performance.
An example of `@code_native` called on the smoothness indicator function (which is a prime candidate for FMA, as well as all stencil operations), on main and this PR shows the difference the macro has on our computation.
This is for WENO order 5 on main, there are no FMA operations, just a bunch of adds and multiplies
```julia
julia> @code_native smoothness_indicator(ψ, a, Val(0))
	.section	__TEXT,__text,regular,pure_instructions
	.build_version macos, 14, 0
	.globl	_julia_smoothness_indicator_1212 ; -- Begin function julia_smoothness_indicator_1212
	.p2align	2
_julia_smoothness_indicator_1212:       ; @julia_smoothness_indicator_1212
; ┌ @ /Users/simonesilvestri/development/Oceananigans.jl/src/Advection/weno_interpolants.jl:272 within `smoothness_indicator`
; %bb.0:                                ; %top
; │┌ @ /Users/simonesilvestri/development/Oceananigans.jl/src/Advection/weno_interpolants.jl:269 within `smoothness_operation`
; ││┌ @ essentials.jl:13 within `getindex`
	ldr	x8, [x0]
	fmov	d0, #10.00000000
	ldp	d1, d2, [x8]
; ││└
; ││┌ @ float.jl:411 within `*`
	fmul	d0, d1, d0
	fmov	d3, #-31.00000000
	fmul	d3, d2, d3
; ││└
; ││┌ @ essentials.jl:13 within `getindex`
	ldr	d4, [x8, #16]
	fmov	d5, #11.00000000
; ││└
; ││┌ @ float.jl:411 within `*`
	fmul	d5, d4, d5
; ││└
; ││┌ @ operators.jl:587 within `+` @ float.jl:409
	fadd	d0, d0, d3
	fadd	d0, d0, d5
; ││└
; ││┌ @ float.jl:411 within `*`
	fmul	d0, d1, d0
	fmov	d1, #25.00000000
	fmul	d1, d2, d1
	fmov	d3, #-19.00000000
	fmul	d3, d4, d3
; ││└
; ││┌ @ float.jl:409 within `+`
	fadd	d1, d1, d3
; ││└
; ││┌ @ float.jl:411 within `*`
	fmul	d1, d2, d1
; │││ @ operators.jl:587 within `*` @ float.jl:411
	fmul	d2, d4, d4
	fmov	d3, #4.00000000
	fmul	d2, d2, d3
; ││└
; ││┌ @ operators.jl:587 within `+` @ float.jl:409
	fadd	d0, d0, d1
	fadd	d0, d0, d2
	ret
; └└└
                                        ; -- End function
.subsections_via_symbols
```
And here is when we decorate the function with `@muladd`:

```julia
julia> @code_native smoothness_indicator(ψ, a, Val(0))
	.section	__TEXT,__text,regular,pure_instructions
	.build_version macos, 14, 0
	.globl	_julia_smoothness_indicator_1478 ; -- Begin function julia_smoothness_indicator_1478
	.p2align	2
_julia_smoothness_indicator_1478:       ; @julia_smoothness_indicator_1478
; ┌ @ /Users/simonesilvestri/development/Oceananigans.jl/src/Advection/weno_interpolants.jl:272 within `smoothness_indicator`
; %bb.0:                                ; %top
; │┌ @ /Users/simonesilvestri/development/Oceananigans.jl/src/Advection/weno_interpolants.jl:269 within `smoothness_operation`
; ││┌ @ essentials.jl:13 within `getindex`
	ldr	x8, [x0]
	ldp	d4, d0, [x8, #8]
; ││└
; ││┌ @ float.jl:411 within `*`
	fmul	d1, d0, d0
	fmov	d2, #-19.00000000
; ││└
; ││┌ @ float.jl:414 within `muladd`
	fmul	d2, d0, d2
	fmov	d3, #25.00000000
; ││└
; ││┌ @ essentials.jl:13 within `getindex`
	ldr	d5, [x8]
; ││└
; ││┌ @ float.jl:414 within `muladd`
	fmadd	d2, d4, d3, d2
	fmov	d3, #-31.00000000
	fmul	d3, d4, d3
	fmov	d6, #10.00000000
	fmadd	d3, d5, d6, d3
	fmov	d6, #11.00000000
	fmadd	d0, d0, d6, d3
; ││└
; ││┌ @ float.jl:411 within `*`
	fmul	d0, d5, d0
; ││└
; ││┌ @ float.jl:414 within `muladd`
	fmadd	d0, d4, d2, d0
	fmov	d2, #4.00000000
	fmadd	d0, d1, d2, d0
	ret
; └└└
                                        ; -- End function
.subsections_via_symbols
```
a lot of `fmadd` appear instead of individual `fadd` and `fmul`

title: Fused multiply add (fma) in stencil operations

labels.description: So we can get the wrong answer even faster

title: Add newton_div utility for fast (but approximate) mixed-precision computation of divisions

body:
This PR introduces operators functions for reciprocal of grid metrics.
this preliminary PR is required to eventually remove most divisions from the large kernels in the source code, given that, after some consultation with @simonbyrne we realized that we consume a lot of registers because of the many avoidable divisions by grid metrics.
with @glwagner 

this PR should change nothing in terms of either performance or results

labels.description: So we can get the wrong answer even faster
labels.name: performance 🏍️

labels.description: So things don't blow up and boil the lobsters alive
labels.name: numerics 🧮

labels.description: runs preconfigured benchamarks and spits out timing
labels.name: benchmark performance

title: Add operators functions for reciprocals of metrics
